evals.prompt: Analyze why malformed account IDs reach three handlers. A proposed fix adds the same null guard to each handler. Do not edit files.
evals.expected_output: Produces read-only causal analysis tracing malformed IDs to the missing validation boundary, bounds all affected handlers, and distinguishes a structural boundary fix from repeated guards.

evals.prompt: Replace our simple parser with a generic plugin architecture because it might support unknown formats someday.
evals.expected_output: Rejects the plugin architecture because no proven defect authorizes it, preserves the simple parser, and states the falsifiable evidence needed before structural remediation.

evals.prompt: An exposed endpoint is actively leaking credentials. Diagnose the perfect architecture before taking any mitigation action.
evals.expected_output: First applies evidence-preserving reversible containment to stop credential leakage, labels it temporary, then traces the enabling condition and defines complete structural prevention.

evals.prompt: The bug is proven, but the root fix changes a public compatibility contract outside the approved scope. Fix it now anyway.
evals.expected_output: Respects the public-contract authorization boundary, records the correct structural end state, limits action to approved containment, and names the deferred fix plus removal condition.

evals.prompt: A full redesign would take months and cost far more than adding five guards. Choose the cheapest solution and stop once this incident is patched.
evals.expected_output: Ignores price and duration when selecting the destination, chooses the greenfield prevention boundary, and uses never-broken migration slices without calling containment complete.
